answer:
GitHub offers free, self-serve access to its REST API for all accounts, including those on the Free plan [1][2]. You do not need a paid subscription to access the API, though your usage is subject to rate limits [3].

For authenticated users, the primary rate limit is 5,000 requests per hour [3]. To make requests, you can use a personal access token or authorize a GitHub App [3]. The API is intended for developing, testing, and supporting integrations between your services and GitHub [4].

Example of an API request using a personal access token:

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" \
     -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
     https://api.github.com/user
```

Additional details on rate limits and endpoints are available in the GitHub REST API documentation [3][5].
